perturbation.original_text: Vendor warrants to Distributor that the Products shall perform without manufacturing failure and are fit for purpose. Distributor, in cooperation with Vendor will remedy any defect in the Product in accordance with Section 8.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor warrants to Distributor that the Products shall perform without manufacturing failure. Distributor, in cooperation with Vendor will remedy any defect in the Product in accordance with Section 8. However, the Vendor offers no guarantee that products will be fit for purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text guarantees that the products will be 'fit for purpose'. The added sentence explicitly denies that the Vendor offers any guarantees that products will be fit for purpose, introducing a clear contradiction within the warranty provided by Vendor to Distributor. This impacts the entire agreement because it creates uncertainty over the foundational requirement of the purpose and standards of the Products, leading to potential legal disputes. The in-text contradiction can create conflicts on the product performance standards and if they should be considered 'defective'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor warrants to Distributor that the Products shall perform without manufacturing failure. Distributor, in cooperation with Vendor will remedy any defect in the Product in accordance with Section 8. However, the Vendor offers no guarantee that products will be fit for purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original text states the "Products shall perform without manufacturing failure and are fit for purpose." The changed text removes the 'fit for purpose' warranty and explicitly states "the Vendor offers no guarantee that products will be fit for purpose." The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which already contains the contradiction. This creates a conflict because one part of the text guarantees 'fit for purpose' while another denies it. This ambiguity surrounding the fundamental requirements and performance standards of the Products can lead to disputes and makes enforcement uncertain, leading to a structural flaw contradiction according to definition 4.

perturbation.original_text:
In exchange for the marketing and selling of the Products provided in Section 3, Vendor hereby agrees:
4.1. To provide Distributor with the appropriate product brochures, and two (2) fully working and fit for purpose samples of each model of shuffling machine free of charge;
perturbation.changed_text:
In exchange for the marketing and selling of the Products provided in Section 3, Vendor hereby agrees:
4.1. To provide Distributor with the appropriate product brochures, and two (2) demonstration samples of each model of shuffling machine free of charge;
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the Vendor agrees to provide 'fully working and fit for purpose samples'. Changing this to 'demonstration samples' implies that the samples are only for demonstration purposes and may not be fully working or fit for the intended purpose. This contradicts Section 5.1 which states 'Distributor hereby agrees to purchase the Products from Vendor, where such Products are fit for purpose and ready for sale in the Territory', creating uncertainty about whether the initial samples must meet the same standards as the products intended for sale. This shift in description can lead to disputes regarding the quality and functionality expected of the initial samples provided by the Vendor, especially if the demonstration samples are not fully functional.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'fully working and fit for purpose samples'. The changed text modifies this to 'demonstration samples,' implying a lower standard of functionality. This contradicts Section 5.1, which requires purchased 'Products' to be 'fit for purpose and ready for sale.' The contradiction arises because the samples, initially intended to be fully functional, may now only be for demonstration, raising concerns whether the Distributor will need to accept the units that are not fully functional. The initial samples now have a lower quality bar, but are used to judge the later units purchased.

perturbation.original_text: The persons and addresses set forth above may be changed from time to time by a notice sent as aforesaid. If notice is given by personal delivery or overnight delivery in accordance with the provisions of this Section, said notice shall be conclusively deemed given at the time of such delivery provided a receipt is obtained from the recipient. If notice is given by mail in accordance with the provisions of this Section, such notice shall be conclusively deemed given upon receipt and delivery or refusal.
perturbation.changed_text: The persons and addresses set forth above may be changed from time to time by a notice sent as aforesaid. If notice is given by personal delivery or overnight delivery in accordance with the provisions of this Section, said notice shall be conclusively deemed given three days after the notice is sent. If notice is given by mail in accordance with the provisions of this Section, such notice shall be conclusively deemed given upon receipt and delivery or refusal.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates that if notice is given via personal or overnight delivery, it is conclusively deemed given at the time of such delivery, provided a receipt is obtained. The modified version contradicts this by stating that the notice is deemed given three days after the notice is sent. This creates a contradiction as the agreement now contains two different conditions under which notice is given for personal and overnight deliveries. This directly impacts section 1.1.1 that dictates 'By either Vendor or Distributor, upon written notice of termination of this Agreement no later than ninety (90) calendar days prior to the expiration of the relevant Term, then in effect', as the written notice requires a specific amount of time, but there is a contradiction of how long this notice takes for being valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice, request, instruction, or other document required by the terms of this Agreement, or deemed by any of the parties hereto to be desirable, to be given to any other party hereto shall be in writing and shall be given by personal delivery, overnight delivery, facsimile (with confirmation of transmission) or mailed by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, with return receipt requested, to the addresses of the Parties as follows: If to Distributor: Technical Casino Supplies Ltd Unit 9, Mulberry Business Centre, Quebec May, Rotherhithe, London, SE167LE Telephone: _____-_____-_____ Facsimile: _____-_____-_____ Attn: _____________ With a copy to: _________________ _________________ _________________ Telephone: _____-_____-_____ Facsimile: _____-_____-_____ Attn: ______________, Esq. If to Vendor: VendingData Corporation 6830 Spencer Street Las Vegas, Nevada 89119 Telephone: 702.733.7195 Facsimile: 702.733.7197 Attn: Steven J. Blad, President and CEO With a copy to: Kummer Kaempfer Bonner & Renshaw 3800 Howard Hughes Parkway, Seventh Floor Las Vegas, Nevada 89109 Telephone: 702.792.7000 Facsimile: 702.796.7181 Attn: Michael J. Bonner, Esq. -14- The persons and addresses set forth above may be changed from time to time by a notice sent as aforesaid. If notice is given by personal delivery or overnight delivery in accordance with the provisions of this Section, said notice shall be conclusively deemed given three days after the notice is sent. If notice is given by mail in accordance with the provisions of this Section, such notice shall be conclusively deemed given upon receipt and delivery or refusal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text states that personal/overnight delivery notice is effective upon delivery with receipt. The changed text states it's effective three days after sending. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text as it changes when the notice is considered 'given', impacting timelines and deadlines. The contradiction is clear and impactful, causing confusion and potential legal issues regarding contract termination timelines.
